assessments.status_rationale: Today’s coverage highlights uncertainty and ongoing payment of tariffs post–Supreme Court ruling, but it does not clearly document a CBP CSMS message or Federal Register notice establishing a formal refund/administrative-relief process with a filing mechanism and effective date. Deadline remains in the future.

News items discuss tariff strategy and market uncertainty after the court loss, but there is no clear evidence that the White House/USTR/Commerce has transmitted proposed legislation to Congress to create/expand executive authority for an across-the-board import surcharge. Deadline remains in the future.

No bullet indicates the EU/Canada/Mexico/China/Japan/South Korea has filed a WTO request for consultations explicitly challenging a U.S. across-the-board global tariff. Deadline remains in the future.
No information provided on DRAMeXchange DDR4 8Gb spot pricing or February 2026 averages; the June 2026 window has not yet occurred.

Today’s bullets do not report an announcement by Samsung, SK hynix, or Micron of a material DDR4 curtailment/phase-out explicitly tied to China-led pricing pressure or margin collapse. Deadline remains in the future.
No NASA announcement is cited today regarding rescheduling Artemis II to an earliest launch date no earlier than 2027-06-30; deadline remains in the future.

While there is weather/aviation-related news (e.g., storm coverage and a TSA PreCheck policy reversal), none of the bullets shows Delta, United, or American disclosing a quantified ≥$100M financial impact from the February 2026 East Coast blizzard in an SEC filing or earnings communications. Deadline remains in the future.
No bulletin provides ICE Dutch TTF front-month settlement levels or confirms a settlement above €60/MWh; deadline remains in the future.

No BLS Import/Export Price Index release data is included in today’s bullets, so there is no evidence confirming or refuting ≥+4.0% y/y import-price inflation ex-petroleum; deadline remains in the future.
